title: pedicularis oederi subsp. branchiophylla
intro: Pedicularis oederi subsp. branchiophylla, also known as Alpine Lousewort or Lousewort, is a sub-species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by drawing them from the roots of nearby plants, often heather or grasses. Pedicularis oederi subsp. branchiophylla is used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it beneficial for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is traditionally prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in the production of herbal remedies, such as salves and ointments, to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
